description:
You are working as a junior data-analyst who has just received two quarterly inventory CSV files that need a little housekeeping before they can be imported into the company’s BI tool.

    Starting situation on the VM:
    1.  Directory “/home/user/raw_data/” already exists.
    2.  Inside “/home/user/raw_data/” there are exactly two files:
        •  inventory_q1.csv  
        •  inventory_q2.csv  
       (both files use UTF-8 and UNIX line-endings, LF)
    3.  The two CSVs have identical column layout:
           id,product,quantity
           …three data rows…
       They have NO trailing blank lines.

    Your tasks, in order (keep it to roughly two-to-three shell commands total):

    A.  Create a new directory called “processed_data” directly under “/home/user/”.
        •   If the directory already exists, do NOT delete or recreate it; simply reuse it.

    B.  Move BOTH CSV files from “/home/user/raw_data/” into this new “/home/user/processed_data/” directory.

    C.  In “/home/user/processed_data/”, create a single file called “combined_inventory.csv” that:
        1) Keeps the header line exactly once (taken from inventory_q1.csv).  
        2) Appends all six data rows (three rows from each source file, in their original order).  
        3) Uses the same column order and no extra blank lines.  
        4) Uses UNIX line-endings (LF).

       The resulting file must therefore contain precisely SEVEN lines total (one header + six data rows).

    D.  Still inside “/home/user/processed_data/”, generate a small log file named “processing_log.txt” with the following STRICT format (do not add extra spaces or lines):

        FileName,Rows
        inventory_q1.csv,3
        inventory_q2.csv,3
        combined_inventory.csv,6

       Notes about the log:
       • “Rows” is the count of data rows only (header excluded).
       • Lines are separated with a single LF character.
       • No leading or trailing whitespace on any line.

    Constraints / verification criteria that the automated grader will check:
    • The directory “/home/user/processed_data/” exists.
    • “/home/user/processed_data/combined_inventory.csv” exists and has exactly 7 lines with the correct order and content.
    • “/home/user/processed_data/processing_log.txt” exists and matches the exact three-line format shown above.
    • The original “/home/user/raw_data/” directory no longer contains the two CSV files (they must have been moved, not copied).

    Only standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities available in a typical Debian/Ubuntu base image may be used.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data for automated grading.
    NOTHING here should appear in the public task description above.
    -->

    Initial filesystem state BEFORE the agent starts:
    ├── /home/user/raw_data/
    │   ├── inventory_q1.csv
    │   └── inventory_q2.csv
    └── (no /home/user/processed_data/ directory yet)

    Contents of the initial CSV files:

    /home/user/raw_data/inventory_q1.csv
    ------------------------------------
    id,product,quantity
    1,Apples,50
    2,Bananas,75
    3,Carrots,40

    /home/user/raw_data/inventory_q2.csv
    ------------------------------------
    id,product,quantity
    4,Dates,60
    5,Eggplants,30
    6,Flour,90


    Expected filesystem state AFTER a correct solution:

    Directory structure:
    /home/user/
      ├── raw_data/                     (directory still exists, now empty)
      └── processed_data/
            ├── inventory_q1.csv        (moved file, unchanged)
            ├── inventory_q2.csv        (moved file, unchanged)
            ├── combined_inventory.csv  (newly created)
            └── processing_log.txt      (newly created)

    Combined CSV expected byte-for-byte content
    (/home/user/processed_data/combined_inventory.csv):

    id,product,quantity
    1,Apples,50
    2,Bananas,75
    3,Carrots,40
    4,Dates,60
    5,Eggplants,30
    6,Flour,90

    Processing log expected byte-for-byte content
    (/home/user/processed_data/processing_log.txt):

    FileName,Rows
    inventory_q1.csv,3
    inventory_q2.csv,3
    combined_inventory.csv,6
